content: Newton's three laws of motion: (1) An object at rest stays at rest unless acted upon by a force, (2) F=ma — force equals mass times acceleration, (3) Every action has an equal and opposite reaction. These laws form the foundation of classical mechanics.

The Standard Model of particle physics describes 17 fundamental particles: 6 quarks (up, down, charm, strange, top, bottom), 6 leptons (electron, muon, tau + their neutrinos), 4 gauge bosons (photon, W, Z, gluon), and the Higgs boson. It explains electromagnetic, weak, and strong forces but not gravity.

DNA replication is semi-conservative: each new DNA molecule contains one original strand and one newly synthesized strand. The process involves helicase (unwinds), primase (RNA primer), DNA polymerase III (synthesis 5'→3'), and ligase (joins Okazaki fragments on the lagging strand).

The Krebs cycle (citric acid cycle) occurs in the mitochondrial matrix and produces 2 ATP, 6 NADH, 2 FADH2, and 4 CO2 per glucose molecule. Combined with glycolysis and oxidative phosphorylation, one glucose yields approximately 30-32 ATP.

Thermodynamics has four laws: Zeroth (thermal equilibrium is transitive), First (energy conservation: ΔU = Q - W), Second (entropy of an isolated system never decreases), Third (entropy approaches zero as temperature approaches absolute zero).

The periodic table organizes 118 elements by atomic number. Key patterns: electronegativity increases left→right and bottom→top (fluorine is most electronegative at 3.98), atomic radius increases right→left and top→bottom, ionization energy follows electronegativity trends.

General relativity (Einstein, 1915) describes gravity as curvature of spacetime caused by mass-energy. Key predictions all confirmed: gravitational lensing, time dilation near massive objects (GPS satellites must correct for this), gravitational waves (detected 2015 by LIGO), and black holes.

CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing works by guiding the Cas9 nuclease to a specific DNA sequence using a guide RNA (gRNA). Cas9 creates a double-strand break, which the cell repairs via non-homologous end joining (NHEJ, creates indels) or homology-directed repair (HDR, precise edits with a template).

Quantum mechanics principles: wave-particle duality (de Broglie), uncertainty principle (Heisenberg: ΔxΔp ≥ ℏ/2), superposition (particles exist in multiple states simultaneously), entanglement (correlated particles regardless of distance), and tunneling (particles can pass through potential barriers).

The electromagnetic spectrum spans from radio waves (λ > 1m, f < 300MHz) through microwaves, infrared, visible light (380-700nm), ultraviolet, X-rays, to gamma rays (λ < 10pm, f > 30EHz). All are transverse waves traveling at c = 3×10⁸ m/s in vacuum. Energy is proportional to frequency: E = hf.

Plate tectonics: Earth's lithosphere is divided into ~15 major tectonic plates floating on the asthenosphere. Plate boundaries are divergent (mid-ocean ridges, new crust), convergent (subduction zones, mountains), or transform (lateral sliding, earthquakes). Convection currents in the mantle drive plate motion.

The Hubble constant (H₀ ≈ 70 km/s/Mpc) measures the expansion rate of the universe. Hubble's Law states v = H₀d — galaxies recede at velocities proportional to their distance. The universe is approximately 13.8 billion years old, composed of ~68% dark energy, ~27% dark matter, and ~5% ordinary matter.

Machine learning loss functions serve different purposes: MSE (regression, penalizes large errors), cross-entropy (classification, measures probability distribution divergence), hinge loss (SVM, maximum margin), KL divergence (generative models, distribution matching), focal loss (imbalanced classification, down-weights easy examples).

The central dogma of molecular biology: DNA → RNA → Protein. Transcription (DNA→mRNA) occurs in the nucleus using RNA polymerase. Translation (mRNA→protein) occurs at ribosomes in the cytoplasm. Reverse transcriptase (in retroviruses like HIV) can convert RNA→DNA, a key exception.

Shannon's information theory: entropy H = -Σ p(x)log₂p(x) measures information content in bits. A fair coin has H=1 bit. Channel capacity C = B·log₂(1+SNR) (Shannon-Hartley theorem). Data compression cannot exceed entropy (source coding theorem). Error-free communication is possible up to channel capacity (noisy channel theorem).

Organic chemistry functional groups determine reactivity: hydroxyl (-OH, alcohols), carbonyl (C=O, aldehydes/ketones), carboxyl (-COOH, acids), amino (-NH₂, amines), phosphate (-PO₄, DNA/ATP), sulfhydryl (-SH, disulfide bonds in proteins). Nucleophilic substitution (SN1/SN2) and elimination (E1/E2) are key reaction mechanisms.

Neural network architectures: CNNs use convolutional layers for spatial pattern recognition (images), RNNs/LSTMs process sequential data (text, time series), Transformers use self-attention for parallel sequence processing (GPT, BERT), GANs pit generator vs discriminator for generation, Diffusion models learn to denoise for high-quality generation.

The Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium states that allele frequencies remain constant in a population when: no mutation, no selection, random mating, no genetic drift (infinite population), and no gene flow. The equation p² + 2pq + q² = 1 predicts genotype frequencies from allele frequencies p and q.

Fourier analysis decomposes any periodic function into a sum of sines and cosines: f(x) = a₀/2 + Σ(aₙcos(nx) + bₙsin(nx)). The FFT algorithm computes the Discrete Fourier Transform in O(n log n) instead of O(n²). Applications: signal processing, image compression (JPEG uses DCT), audio analysis, solving PDEs.

Statistical hypothesis testing: null hypothesis (H₀) assumes no effect. p-value is the probability of observing data at least as extreme as the result, assuming H₀ is true. Common tests: t-test (means comparison), chi-squared (categorical data), ANOVA (multiple group means), Mann-Whitney U (non-parametric). Type I error (false positive) = α, Type II error (false negative) = β.
